title: Evangelion Platinum Perfect questions
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion Platinum Perfect questions             Quote Post by Dave_k » Mon May 02, 2011 5:24 pm New guy here, hope this is the right section. So i wanted to get this DVD set, i like that it comes with the extra disc, and the tin. finding one for a reasonable price has turned out to be kinda hard. i've talked to a few site that have it, but claims it's back-ordered. now, i was under the assumption that it was out of production, and more weren't coming in... Anyone here able to shed some light on this? If it's gonna be released again i'd love to pre-order it, finding this set for an affordable price is killing me. -thanks

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon May 02, 2011 5:54 pm I believe all versions of Platinum are out of print, including the most recent "holiday" edition. The holiday edition has the exact same discs, just in a different, more compact case. It might be easier to track down. http://www.evamonkey.com/limited-edition-holiday-special/

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Mon May 02, 2011 8:35 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: I believe all versions of Platinum are out of print, including the most recent "holiday" edition. The holiday edition has the exact same discs, just in a different, more compact case. It might be easier to track down. http://www.evamonkey.com/limited-edition-holiday-special/

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon May 02, 2011 8:59 pm Stryker wrote: I like that case art. [/ShippingR/S]

[]:             Quote Post by Dave_k » Mon May 09, 2011 4:31 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: I believe all versions of Platinum are out of print, including the most recent "holiday" edition. The holiday edition has the exact same discs, just in a different, more compact case. It might be easier to track down. http://www.evamonkey.com/limited-edition-holiday-special/

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Mon May 09, 2011 4:48 pm Dave_k wrote: hey, thanks for the info man. i have another question if you dont mind me throwing another at ya. would you consider it worth it (that is, for someone who would like the platinum release, but the special fatures and booklets too) for me to buy the individual platinum releases, plus the one that came with the series box? I thought this would be a nice set to build up as it has the platinum release, the extras (yeah, yeah, no one likes them), and the booklets. plus it's not astronomically expensive. no? also, i loved your panel at AnimeBoston, glad i could make it there.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon May 09, 2011 10:03 pm Dave_k wrote: would you consider it worth it for me to buy the individual platinum releases?

[]:             Quote Post by Dave_k » Mon May 09, 2011 10:10 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Because they're a limited-set-specific extra, I had those booklets transcribed, so you can read their contents online. As svenge said, you can wait for an entire set of the original Platinum release to pop up on eBay, but my suggestion is still the Thinpak. If you are set on having all of the DVD extras, grab the "holiday" version, and then just read the booklets online. Thanks dude, glad you enjoyed it. Did you go to Evangelion Is Serious Business , or The Evangelion Omake Box ?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon May 09, 2011 10:36 pm Dave_k wrote: we went to the 'Evangelion is Serious Business' panel. it was pretty interesting for me. i was actually up front just using my camera to snap pictures of any interesting slides lol. we wanted to go to the 'Omake Box' panel, but got held up at something else.

[]:             Quote Post by Alastor » Mon May 09, 2011 11:01 pm Might I suggest holiday? It's about 40 bucks on rightstuf. Plus, if you choose the Vol. 1, they sell the rest of Platinum pretty cheap.

[]:             Quote Post by Dave_k » Mon May 09, 2011 11:48 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: No prob. At least you made it to the better of the two. Omake Box went okay, Serious Business went really well I think. Or you could be like me... and buy every version..

[]:             Quote Post by NeverKnowsBest » Wed May 11, 2011 6:09 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Thanks dude, glad you enjoyed it. Did you go to Evangelion Is Serious Business , or The Evangelion Omake Box ?

[]:             Quote Post by Hawq » Wed May 11, 2011 2:53 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: I believe all versions of Platinum are out of print, including the most recent "holiday" edition. The holiday edition has the exact same discs, just in a different, more compact case. It might be easier to track down. http://www.evamonkey.com/limited-edition-holiday-special/

[]:             Quote Post by Santo » Wed May 11, 2011 9:10 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Or you could be like me... and buy every version..

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed May 11, 2011 9:53 pm Santo wrote: How many different sets are there? What are they called? hahaa

[]:             Quote Post by Alastor » Wed May 11, 2011 10:58 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: http://wiki.evageeks.org/North_American_Video_Releases I have pretty much everything on this list.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed May 11, 2011 11:07 pm Alastor wrote: Eva Monkey: the monkey's whose house is made of Eva crap.

[]:             Quote Post by Alastor » Wed May 11, 2011 11:14 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: No, just one shelving unit...

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu May 12, 2011 7:00 am Alastor wrote: ...Intervention?

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Thu May 12, 2011 12:23 pm That's some pretty good stuff there. From what I can identify on the top shelf, you've got the original Perfect Collection , the Platinum singles w/ box, the Platinum Complete thinpak set, the Holiday Edition brick, 2 or 3 copies of Manga's Death+Rebirth and End of Evangelion (including the boxed set version), the ADV Director's Cut singles, 1.01 DVD, 1.11 DVD and BD, the R2J Theatrical Version DTS Collector's Edition , the R2J Puchi/Petit Eva (Evangelion@School) DVD collection, the R2J Music DVD, and the R2J "remix" DVD. There were a couple things I couldn't make out, but I think I got the others right. Since you took that picture in February (from the title of the file) I bet you picked up 2.22 on both DVD and BD as well (especially since the 2.22 DVD was pre-ordering for $9.99 at many places before its release.) I certainly can't compare, primarily because I really never cared for character goods or print items (as I'd rather buy more anime titles instead.) I am impressed that you have all the storyboard books (on the bottom shelf), though.
summary: []: Evangelion Platinum Perfect questions             Quote Post by Dave_k » Mon May 02, 2011 5:24 pm New guy here, hope this is the right section. So i wanted to get this DVD set, i like that it comes with the extra disc, and the tin. finding one for a reasonable price has turned out to be kinda hard. i've talked to a few site that have it, but claims it's back-ordered. now, i was under the assumption that it was out of production,
